sections.paragraph:
ἔκγονος, ον prim. sense ‘born of, sprung from’; usu. subst. ὁ, ἡ ἔκγονος (ἐκγίγνομαι ‘be born of’; Hom. et al.; ins, pap; JosAs 29:11 cod. Ἀτῷ ἐ. [p. 85, 15 Bat.]; s. Mayser 228; Orpheus 10 Denis p. 165) τὰ ἔκγονα gener. descendants (Diod S 4, 82, 4; Diog. L. 3, 81; LXX, Philo; Jos., Ant. 11, 2; 111) spec. grandchildren (Hesych. τέκνα τέκνων; OGI 740, 4f [I b.c.] τ. τέκνων καὶ τ. ἐκγόνων; Dio Chrys. 21 [38], 21; Chariton 2, 9, 2; Herm. Wr. 10, 14b; Ath., R. 611, 18 ἐγγόνων; s. L-S-J-M entry ἔγγονος). The ancient versions understand it in the latter sense 1 Ti 5:4. Hm 9, 9 v.l. for θυγάτηρ.—B. 112; 121. DELG s.v. γίγνομαι B 223. M-M. TW. Sv.

prim. prim. = primarily

usu. usu. = usually

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Denis Denis = AD., Fragmenta pseudepigraphorum quae supersunt Graeca—Lists 2 beg., 5, 6

gener. gener. = generally

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

Hm Hm = Mandates

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 300
